newsSince: On June 17, Panasonic confirmed its De Soto (Kansas) and Japanese lines will be repurposed to make AI data-center batteries, with ~$2.2-3B capex through FY2029 and a data-center sales target of $6.2B by FY2029. This is incremental execution on the June 8 Investors Day plan (polymer-cap >2x capacity, CBUs, supercapacitors) already captured in the prior verdict, not net-new information.

sources.label: Panasonic De Soto shift to AI data-center batteries (Kansas Reflector)

verdicts.rankRationale: June 17 De Soto/Japan line conversion executes the June 8 Investors Day plan (CBUs, supercaps, polymer-cap >2x) the prior verdict already down-ranked for being consumed by the market. Reinforces the passives/power thesis but adds no fresh hidden alpha; rerating already priced. Hold at 9.
verdicts.bull: Polymer-tantalum/CBU/supercap capacity doubling directly serves AI server power stability; explicit $6.2B DC target.
verdicts.neutral: Thesis confirmed but now front-page AI-infra consensus; +104% YTD.
verdicts.bear: Residual alpha largely consumed; conglomerate dilutes pure passives leverage.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Data-center battery/CBU ramp and capacity doubling
verdicts.primaryRisk: Crowded consensus; capex execution and DC demand timing
